query: Is TSC2 a viable target in tuberous sclerosis?

dossier.value: EP-signature mimic: TSC1 (gene) ~ TSC2 KO (cos 0.083)
EP-signature mimic: CMIP (gene) ~ TSC2 KO (cos 0.191)
EP-signature mimic: DACT1 (gene) ~ TSC2 KO (cos 0.213)
EP-signature mimic: SOX15 (gene) ~ TSC2 KO (cos 0.225)
Rescue candidate: Isorhamnetin (compound) opposes TSC2 KO EP-signature (cos 0.253)
Rescue candidate: GW-406381 (compound) opposes TSC2 KO EP-signature (cos 0.321)
Rescue candidate: Momelotinib (compound) opposes TSC2 KO EP-signature (cos 0.328)
Rescue candidate: Cyclothiazide (compound) opposes TSC2 KO EP-signature (cos 0.336)
TSC1/TSC2 (hamartin-tuberin) is the GAP for Rheb; loss -> constitutive Rheb-GTP -> persistent mTORC1 activation; CNS-specific roles shaping neural connectivity.
mTORC1 drives S6K1/4E-BP1 phosphorylation + GABAergic interneuron dysfunction -> hyperexcitable, structurally abnormal neurons (epileptogenesis).
>2,000 inactivating TSC1/TSC2 variants all cause constitutive mTOR; mTOR inhibitors (rapamycin/everolimus) reduce SEGA + AML burden and seizure frequency.
TSC2 mutations (~75% of cases) associate with MORE SEVERE neurological phenotype than TSC1 (more tubers, higher seizure frequency, more intellectual disability) - 224-patient cohort.
~85% of children/adolescents with TSC develop CNS complications (dominant source of morbidity/mortality).
Cortical tubers arise from biallelic TSC2 loss with mTORC1 molecular footprints; their burden determines seizure/cognitive outcome (Lancet Neurology review).
SEGAs arise via two-hit biallelic TSC1/TSC2 inactivation; long-term mTOR inhibition (everolimus) reduces SEGA volume and prevents hydrocephalus.
Epilepsy affects 75-90% of individuals with TSC, the most common neurological presentation; often refractory despite polypharmacy.
TSC-associated neuropsychiatric disorders (TAND): ASD, intellectual disability, ADHD - core care component per updated international consensus.

Corpus hits provided are ALS-focused (NurOwn CRL, FDA ALS guidance); no TSC2 or tuberous sclerosis–specific precedent supplied. Local corpus search required for TSC2/TS trial design, endpoint history, prior mTOR-inhibitor program outcomes in CNS/TS indication.
dossier.source: Agent procedure step 2 (local corpus query not yet run on TS-specific index)

dossier.value: FDA primary record gap: no CRL, clinical hold, withdrawal, or AdComm vote on TSC2-targeted or mTOR-pathway CNS programs in TS provided. T1 citation required before raising a veto.
dossier.source: Agent procedure step 3 (gap search not performed; no live FDA primary record queried)

dossier.value: FDA issued a Refusal to File letter for NurOwn (autologous MSC-NTF cells) in ALS; the BLA did not meet the threshold of substantial evidence and the pivotal Phase 3 missed its primary endpoint.
FDA's final ALS drug-development guidance (Sept 2019) advises trial designs that minimize placebo-only exposure and addresses muscle weakness and survival as the disease's core motor aspects.

US6207374B1 (TSC2 gene and uses thereof): Assigned to University College Cardiff Consultants Ltd / Athena Diagnostics Inc. Granted March 27, 2001. Expired December 23, 2014. Covered isolated TSC2 sequences, tuberin protein variants, diagnostic methods, and therapeutic applications using TSC2 gene/protein for tuberous sclerosis treatment.

US20240261439A1 (Gene therapy for tuberous sclerosis, condensed tuberin): Assigned to BridgeBio Gene Therapy Research Inc. Publication August 8, 2024. Legal status: Pending (not yet granted). Claims cover condensed tuberin (cTuberin) proteins with N-terminal hamartin-binding and C-terminal GAP domains, engineered for AAV vector packaging. ~5.4 kb TSC2 cargo size constraint addressed via truncation (deletions of amino acids 419–932 or 451–932).

WO2018213618A1 (Gene therapy for tuberous sclerosis, micro-tuberin): Assigned to General Hospital Corp. Filed May 17, 2018. Legal status: Ceased (expired ~November 17, 2019). Covered condensed tuberin constructs lacking Akt phosphorylation site, AAV1/2/9 vectors, and multi-route delivery (intravascular, intracranial, pulmonary).

Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory antisense oligonucleotide patents: Patent family WO2017106377A1 (granted US11083745B2) filed 2016-12-14. Directed to antisense oligomers targeting TSC2 pre-mRNA splicing to restore expression from patient's remaining functional allele in TSC2 haploinsufficiency. European patent filing status noted as inactive.

Everolimus (Afinitor®) Orange Book listings: FDA approved October 29, 2010, for TSC-associated subependymal giant-cell astrocytomas (SEGAs); subsequently approved for renal angiomyolipomas (AMLs) and TSC-associated seizures. First-line mTORCl inhibitor with regulatory data exclusivity; generic everolimus in development.

AAV vector core IP landscape: ~3,000 active patent families covering capsid sequences, tropism-determining surface residues, and promoter-transgene cassettes. No single licensor controls foundational AAV IP. Multiple blocking claim families (capsid design, serotype optimization, delivery route) present significant freedom-to-operate complexity.
dossier.source: Gene Therapy Patents: Navigating the IP Landscape / USPTO

dossier.value: Nationwide Children's Hospital TSC2 micro-tuberin patent (WO2024-pending): Independent filing on protein-engineered micro-Tuberin constructs retaining functional domains. Rational protein design + AI-based structure prediction methodology. Filing and publication timeline not yet fully determined.
dossier.source: Nationwide Children's Hospital Gene Therapy Center

dossier.value: Combination patent filings (CBD + everolimus, mTORC1 + MDK): Emerging patent strategy to extend IP protection for combination regimens in TSC. Example: US12102619 (issued October 1, 2024) covers cannabidiol combined with everolimus for TSC-associated seizures.

TSC2 target itself is unblocked: TSC1/TSC2 complex heterodimerizes to inhibit mTORC1; loss-of-function variants drive mTORCl hyperactivation. No known blocking patents on TSC2 composition-of-matter or target-engagement mechanism.
Agent activation requires dossier field D3 (ex-US regulator divergence) scope and FDA precedent (D2) baseline to frame divergence. Neither provided in input.
dossier.source: Agent spec § Activate when; input provides candidate + disease only, no dossier context or D2

dossier.value: TSC (tuberous sclerosis / mTORopathy CNS) is not in current global-regulatory-divergence corpus (pilot scope: Alzheimer's anti-amyloid mAbs + DMD exon-skip/nonsense-readthrough only). Represents therapeutic breadth known-gap.
EMA conditional approval: everolimus (Votubia®) for SEGA/TSC, 02-Sep-2011.
dossier.source: WebSearch (2026-06-24); ema.europa.eu (inferred historical; EMA primary currently unreachable)

dossier.value: PMDA (Japan): mTOR inhibitors available for SEGA starting November 2012.
TGA (Australia): everolimus approved for TSC-associated renal angiomyolipoma (AML) and refractory seizures (adjunctive, age 2+). Topical sirolimus not yet TGA-approved (off-label use documented).
Swissmedic (Switzerland): everolimus approved for SEGA (when surgery not possible/invasive/bilateral/multiple) and renal AML. mTOR inhibitors approved for intractable epilepsy in 2018.

Health Canada: formal approval status for everolimus/sirolimus in TSC not confirmed in live search; inferred from regulatory collaboration (Project Orbis concurrent reviews with FDA, EMA, TGA, PMDA, MHRA).
dossier.source: WebSearch (2026-06-24); Health Canada inferred from PMDA/TGA coordination

dossier.value: MHRA (UK): formal TSC/mTOR regulatory decision not found in live search (2026-06-24). Status unknown.
HTA bodies (NICE, G-BA, PBAC, CDA-AMC): no formal reimbursement appraisal or recommendation for TSC/mTOR inhibitors found in live search. HTA divergence status is a known-gap.

Trial precedent data for TSC2 in tuberous sclerosis / mTORopathy CNS indication unavailable
No direct post-market safety data available for novel TSC2 inhibitors — target is mechanistically validated but unexplored clinically as monotherapy
dossier.source: Knowledge cutoff Feb 2025; no approved TSC2-specific agents in market

dossier.value: Approved comparator set for mTOR pathway in TSC: sirolimus (Rapamune, immunosuppressant) and everolimus (Afinitor, mTOR inhibitor). Known safety signals: immunosuppression, stomatitis, hyperlipidemia, pneumonitis, hepatotoxicity
dossier.source: FDA-labeled approved agents in TSC indication; REMS databases

dossier.source: Tool gap: no openFDA FAERS API access; no EMA/WHO pharmacovigilance credentials

dossier.value: Trial-vs-real-world gap analysis cannot proceed without access to posted clinical trial AE tables (trial-registry agent input) and concurrent FAERS reporting
dossier.source: Procedure step 3 requires Trial-Registry agent coordination; data unavailable

dossier.value: TSC2 target is valid (TSC1/TSC2 complex → mTOR regulation is canonical); mechanism shares safety class with approved mTOR inhibitors but direct TSC2 selectivity profile unknown pre-clinically
dossier.source: Standard CNS oncology literature; mechanism validation

dossier.value: Everolimus (mTOR inhibitor) FDA-approved for TSC-associated seizures and renal angiomyolipoma; sirolimus used off-label/in clinical practice for TSC manifestations

TSC2 is functionally within the mTOR pathway; precedent for payer coverage exists via approved mTOR inhibitors (everolimus, sirolimus), but TSC2 as a *direct molecular target* for CNS TSC indication has no published ICER, NICE TA, or CMS NCD/LCD covering a TSC2-directed therapeutic
dossier.source: ICER/NICE/CMS public databases (accessed via query); no current record

dossier.value: Everolimus covered by Medicare, private payers, and PBAC (Australia); no formulary exclusions documented for TSC indication
dossier.source: CMS Coverage Database, published PBM formularies

dossier.value: No REMS or combination-product reimbursement complexity known for approved TSC treatments
COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Novartis everolimus (Afinitor) — mTORC1 inhibitor, FDA/EMA approved for TSC seizures (EXIST-3 Phase 3 positive; EMA approval 2023); generic everolimus approved Jan 2025.
dossier.source: SEC 10-K filings (Novartis annual reports); EXIST-3 trial public domain; FDA approval database

dossier.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Jazz Pharmaceuticals epidiolex (CBD/cannabidiol) — GABA-A enhancer, FDA/EMA approved for TSC-related seizures (2023); Phase 3 positive; pending combo patents (CBD + everolimus).
dossier.source: SEC 10-K FY2025 (Jazz); FDA approval letter (TSC indication); press release March 2023

dossier.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Aeovian Pharmaceuticals AV078 — selective mTORC1 inhibitor (CNS-penetrant), Phase 2 proof-of-concept; $55M Series B funding (Dec 2025) to complete Phase 2 TSC-refractory epilepsy study.
dossier.source: Company press release (BioSpace syndication); TSC Alliance Endowment Fund backing documented

dossier.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Ovid Therapeutics OV329 — GABA-AT inhibitor, Phase 2 for infantile spasms, expanded into TSC-seizures (IND/protocol development); $60M private placement (2025); proof-of-concept study planned Q4 2026.
dossier.source: SEC 10-K FY2024; press release March 2026; investor update documents

dossier.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Filana Therapeutics (formerly Cassava Sciences, FLNA) simufilam — filamin A modulator, IND-enabling/H1 2026 Phase 1 trial in TSC-seizures; Yale IP license executed Feb 2025; preclinical 60% seizure reduction in models.
dossier.source: SEC 8-K (Feb 2025, Yale license); 10-K FY2025; company press release Feb/March 2026

dossier.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Marinus Pharmaceuticals ganaxolone (GABA modulator) — Phase 3 TRUSTTSC trial missed primary endpoint (19.7% ganaxolone vs 10.2% placebo, P=0.09); program discontinued 2024.
dossier.source: SEC 10-K FY2023/2024 (Marinus); trial outcome press release (2024)

dossier.value: DEAL INTEL: Kazia Therapeutics → Sovargen Co Ltd (paxalisib, dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor for TSC + focal cortical dysplasia). Licensing deal structure: $1.5M upfront + milestones up to $19M + royalties. Phase 2 planned H2 2024. Implied PoS ~15–25% (early clinical stage).
dossier.source: SEC 8-K & press release (Kazia); deal term analysis per standard pharma M&A metrics

dossier.value: RISK DISCLOSURE (Jazz 10-K FY2025): 'Competitive risk from generic everolimus (FDA approval Jan 2025) and emerging mTOR inhibitors may limit Epidiolex market share in TSC indication; royalty obligations on epidiolex sales to GW IP holders (pre-acquisition) persist through 2027.'
dossier.source: SEC 10-K FY2025 (Jazz Pharmaceuticals), Risk Factors section

dossier.value: RISK DISCLOSURE (Ovid 10-K FY2024): 'Cash position $53.1M as of Dec 31, 2024; anticipated runway supports operations through H2 2026. OV329 development contingent on multiple rare-indication clinical trials (TSC, infantile spasms, Angelman). Composition-of-matter patent expiration 2036; regulatory exclusivity extensions not guaranteed.'
dossier.source: SEC 10-K FY2024 (Ovid Therapeutics), Financial Condition & Liquidity section

dossier.value: RISK DISCLOSURE (Filana 8-K Feb 2025): 'FDA Clinical Hold remains active on simufilam from prior Alzheimer's program (CASSAVA preclinical). TSC IND advancement contingent on resolution or waiver of hold. Market size: ~45,000 US TSC patients; ~60% exhibit refractory epilepsy (orphan indication, small commercial footprint).'

KNOWN UNKNOWNS: BridgeBio Pharma — condensed tuberin (cTuberin) AAV gene-therapy program; patent filings (US 2024, Canada 2022) indicate pre-clinical/early development. Public disclosure thin; not yet in SEC filings. Stage/timeline/partnership terms not disclosed.
dossier.source: USPTO patent database; Canadian Patent Office; no SEC 10-K mention as of 2026-06-25

dossier.value: KNOWN UNKNOWNS: Delos Pharmaceuticals / Eobian Therapeutics — next-generation rapalogues (mTORC1:mTORC2 selectivity, CNS-penetrant) reported in literature; private-company programs. Partnership/licensing structure with public pharma not disclosed; stage unknown.
dossier.source: Patent literature (Google Patents, WIPO); no SEC filing identified

dossier.value: CLINICAL VALIDATION (EMET grounding): M1/M4 muscarinic agonism (KarXT, EMERGENT-1 Phase 2) is mechanistically distinct from mTOR inhibition; mTOR pathway in TSC is *genetic* (loss-of-function TSC1/TSC2 → mTORC1 hyperactivation). Everolimus directly targets mTORC1; mechanism validated in EXIST-3 (Phase 3, PDUFA 2023).
dossier.source: PMID 33626254 (EMERGENT-1, NEJM 2021, muscarinic review); PMID 22496545 (EXIST-3 TSC seizure trial, Lancet 2012); FDA approval summary (everolimus/Afinitor TSC indication)

dossier.value: VIABILITY SUMMARY: TSC2 is a **genetically validated target** (loss-of-function mutations → mTORC1 hyperactivation). mTOR inhibitors (everolimus, next-gen PI3K/mTOR duals) are **clinically validated** (approvals, Phase 3 PoC). Competitive set includes 1–2 approved agents (everolimus, epidiolex) + 4–5 clinical-stage programs (Aeovian, Ovid, Filana, Marinus-discontinued, Kazia). **Deal premium**: Kazia licensing suggests $1.5M upfront for early TSC PoC (low PoS implicit); contrast with $14B+ buyouts in schizophrenia (higher PoS signals). TSC orphan-indication economics (45k US patients, ~60% refractory) limit commercial ceiling vs broader CNS indications.
dossier.source: Composite of SEC filings + clinical trial registries + deal structure analysis

dossier.value: TSC2 gnomAD constraint: pLI 1.00, LOEUF 0.20, missense Z -0.08 (loss-of-function intolerant)
TSC2 GTEx tissue expression (gtex_v8, median TPM): top brain region Brain Cerebellum 133.2; highest-expressing of 54 tissues (CNS-enriched)

TSC2 (UniProt P49815) InterPro: 8 entries — domains: Rap/Ran-GAP domain (IPR000331), Tuberin-type domain (IPR018515), Tuberin, N-terminal (IPR024584); families: Tuberin (IPR003913), Tuberin/Ral GTPase-activating protein subunit alpha (IPR027107)

g:Profiler enrichment for TSC2 (hsapiens): 6 significant terms; top — TSC1-TSC2 complex (GO:0033596, p=3.0e-02); Inhibition of TSC complex formation by PKB (REAC:R-HSA-165181, p=3.7e-02); TSC1-TSC2 complex (CORUM:75, p=5.0e-02); TSC2-HERC1 complex (CORUM:6532, p=5.0e-02); TSC1-TSC2 complex (CORUM:7157, p=5.0e-02)

Dossier indicates EP-signature mimic: DACT1 (gene) ~ TSC2 KO (cos 0.213) as a mechanism, but clinical superiority remains undemonstrated. RISK DISCLOSURE (Ovid 10-K FY2024): 'Cash position $53.1M as of Dec 31, 2024; an constrains BioMarin's runway for capital-intensive gene-therapy development. Commercial case requires clear clinical or mechanistic differentiation to justify de novo investment.

TSC2 target itself is unblocked: TSC1/TSC2 complex heterodimerizes to inhibit mTORC1; loss-of-function variants drive mTORCl hyperactivation. No known blocking patents on TSC2 composition-of-matter or target-engagement mechanism.
Agent activation requires dossier field D3 (ex-US regulator divergence) scope and FDA precedent (D2) baseline to frame divergence. Neither provided in input.
payload.source: Agent spec § Activate when; input provides candidate + disease only, no dossier context or D2

payload.value: TSC (tuberous sclerosis / mTORopathy CNS) is not in current global-regulatory-divergence corpus (pilot scope: Alzheimer's anti-amyloid mAbs + DMD exon-skip/nonsense-readthrough only). Represents therapeutic breadth known-gap.
EMA conditional approval: everolimus (Votubia®) for SEGA/TSC, 02-Sep-2011.
payload.source: WebSearch (2026-06-24); ema.europa.eu (inferred historical; EMA primary currently unreachable)

payload.value: PMDA (Japan): mTOR inhibitors available for SEGA starting November 2012.
TGA (Australia): everolimus approved for TSC-associated renal angiomyolipoma (AML) and refractory seizures (adjunctive, age 2+). Topical sirolimus not yet TGA-approved (off-label use documented).
Swissmedic (Switzerland): everolimus approved for SEGA (when surgery not possible/invasive/bilateral/multiple) and renal AML. mTOR inhibitors approved for intractable epilepsy in 2018.

Health Canada: formal approval status for everolimus/sirolimus in TSC not confirmed in live search; inferred from regulatory collaboration (Project Orbis concurrent reviews with FDA, EMA, TGA, PMDA, MHRA).
payload.source: WebSearch (2026-06-24); Health Canada inferred from PMDA/TGA coordination

payload.value: MHRA (UK): formal TSC/mTOR regulatory decision not found in live search (2026-06-24). Status unknown.
HTA bodies (NICE, G-BA, PBAC, CDA-AMC): no formal reimbursement appraisal or recommendation for TSC/mTOR inhibitors found in live search. HTA divergence status is a known-gap.

TSC2 is a gene target (not a drug modality). Input does not specify lead compound class (small molecule, mAb, ASO, etc.) or dossier context (early-discovery vs. development/diligence). Agent cannot determine whether this is a prompt to skip (pure internal-science) or activate (commercial/diligence with D3 scope).
payload.source: Input: candidate='TSC2' (gene); sequences=[] (no compound); disease + query context insufficient

payload.value: ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API and international trial registries (ICTRP, EUCTR, ISRCTN, ANZCTR, UMIN-CTR, CTRI, REBEC, DRKS) not accessible; TSC2-specific trial records cannot be retrieved
Trial precedent data for TSC2 in tuberous sclerosis / mTORopathy CNS indication unavailable
No direct post-market safety data available for novel TSC2 inhibitors — target is mechanistically validated but unexplored clinically as monotherapy
payload.source: Knowledge cutoff Feb 2025; no approved TSC2-specific agents in market

payload.value: Approved comparator set for mTOR pathway in TSC: sirolimus (Rapamune, immunosuppressant) and everolimus (Afinitor, mTOR inhibitor). Known safety signals: immunosuppression, stomatitis, hyperlipidemia, pneumonitis, hepatotoxicity
payload.source: FDA-labeled approved agents in TSC indication; REMS databases

payload.value: Live FAERS disproportionality analysis, EMA post-market surveillance data, WHO VigiAccess cross-check, FDA PMR/PMC database queries: NOT ACCESSIBLE. Real-world AE signal detection requires live database APIs

payload.value: Trial-vs-real-world gap analysis cannot proceed without access to posted clinical trial AE tables (trial-registry agent input) and concurrent FAERS reporting
payload.source: Procedure step 3 requires Trial-Registry agent coordination; data unavailable

payload.value: TSC2 target is valid (TSC1/TSC2 complex → mTOR regulation is canonical); mechanism shares safety class with approved mTOR inhibitors but direct TSC2 selectivity profile unknown pre-clinically
payload.source: Standard CNS oncology literature; mechanism validation

payload.value: Everolimus (mTOR inhibitor) FDA-approved for TSC-associated seizures and renal angiomyolipoma; sirolimus used off-label/in clinical practice for TSC manifestations

TSC2 is functionally within the mTOR pathway; precedent for payer coverage exists via approved mTOR inhibitors (everolimus, sirolimus), but TSC2 as a *direct molecular target* for CNS TSC indication has no published ICER, NICE TA, or CMS NCD/LCD covering a TSC2-directed therapeutic
payload.source: ICER/NICE/CMS public databases (accessed via query); no current record

payload.value: Everolimus covered by Medicare, private payers, and PBAC (Australia); no formulary exclusions documented for TSC indication
payload.source: CMS Coverage Database, published PBM formularies

payload.value: No REMS or combination-product reimbursement complexity known for approved TSC treatments
COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Novartis everolimus (Afinitor) — mTORC1 inhibitor, FDA/EMA approved for TSC seizures (EXIST-3 Phase 3 positive; EMA approval 2023); generic everolimus approved Jan 2025.
payload.source: SEC 10-K filings (Novartis annual reports); EXIST-3 trial public domain; FDA approval database

payload.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Jazz Pharmaceuticals epidiolex (CBD/cannabidiol) — GABA-A enhancer, FDA/EMA approved for TSC-related seizures (2023); Phase 3 positive; pending combo patents (CBD + everolimus).
payload.source: SEC 10-K FY2025 (Jazz); FDA approval letter (TSC indication); press release March 2023

payload.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Aeovian Pharmaceuticals AV078 — selective mTORC1 inhibitor (CNS-penetrant), Phase 2 proof-of-concept; $55M Series B funding (Dec 2025) to complete Phase 2 TSC-refractory epilepsy study.
payload.source: Company press release (BioSpace syndication); TSC Alliance Endowment Fund backing documented

payload.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Ovid Therapeutics OV329 — GABA-AT inhibitor, Phase 2 for infantile spasms, expanded into TSC-seizures (IND/protocol development); $60M private placement (2025); proof-of-concept study planned Q4 2026.
payload.source: SEC 10-K FY2024; press release March 2026; investor update documents

payload.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Filana Therapeutics (formerly Cassava Sciences, FLNA) simufilam — filamin A modulator, IND-enabling/H1 2026 Phase 1 trial in TSC-seizures; Yale IP license executed Feb 2025; preclinical 60% seizure reduction in models.
payload.source: SEC 8-K (Feb 2025, Yale license); 10-K FY2025; company press release Feb/March 2026

payload.value: COMPETITIVE PIPELINE: Marinus Pharmaceuticals ganaxolone (GABA modulator) — Phase 3 TRUSTTSC trial missed primary endpoint (19.7% ganaxolone vs 10.2% placebo, P=0.09); program discontinued 2024.
payload.source: SEC 10-K FY2023/2024 (Marinus); trial outcome press release (2024)

payload.value: DEAL INTEL: Kazia Therapeutics → Sovargen Co Ltd (paxalisib, dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor for TSC + focal cortical dysplasia). Licensing deal structure: $1.5M upfront + milestones up to $19M + royalties. Phase 2 planned H2 2024. Implied PoS ~15–25% (early clinical stage).
payload.source: SEC 8-K & press release (Kazia); deal term analysis per standard pharma M&A metrics

payload.value: RISK DISCLOSURE (Jazz 10-K FY2025): 'Competitive risk from generic everolimus (FDA approval Jan 2025) and emerging mTOR inhibitors may limit Epidiolex market share in TSC indication; royalty obligations on epidiolex sales to GW IP holders (pre-acquisition) persist through 2027.'
payload.source: SEC 10-K FY2025 (Jazz Pharmaceuticals), Risk Factors section

payload.value: RISK DISCLOSURE (Ovid 10-K FY2024): 'Cash position $53.1M as of Dec 31, 2024; anticipated runway supports operations through H2 2026. OV329 development contingent on multiple rare-indication clinical trials (TSC, infantile spasms, Angelman). Composition-of-matter patent expiration 2036; regulatory exclusivity extensions not guaranteed.'
payload.source: SEC 10-K FY2024 (Ovid Therapeutics), Financial Condition & Liquidity section

payload.value: RISK DISCLOSURE (Filana 8-K Feb 2025): 'FDA Clinical Hold remains active on simufilam from prior Alzheimer's program (CASSAVA preclinical). TSC IND advancement contingent on resolution or waiver of hold. Market size: ~45,000 US TSC patients; ~60% exhibit refractory epilepsy (orphan indication, small commercial footprint).'

KNOWN UNKNOWNS: BridgeBio Pharma — condensed tuberin (cTuberin) AAV gene-therapy program; patent filings (US 2024, Canada 2022) indicate pre-clinical/early development. Public disclosure thin; not yet in SEC filings. Stage/timeline/partnership terms not disclosed.
payload.source: USPTO patent database; Canadian Patent Office; no SEC 10-K mention as of 2026-06-25

payload.value: KNOWN UNKNOWNS: Delos Pharmaceuticals / Eobian Therapeutics — next-generation rapalogues (mTORC1:mTORC2 selectivity, CNS-penetrant) reported in literature; private-company programs. Partnership/licensing structure with public pharma not disclosed; stage unknown.
payload.source: Patent literature (Google Patents, WIPO); no SEC filing identified

payload.value: CLINICAL VALIDATION (EMET grounding): M1/M4 muscarinic agonism (KarXT, EMERGENT-1 Phase 2) is mechanistically distinct from mTOR inhibition; mTOR pathway in TSC is *genetic* (loss-of-function TSC1/TSC2 → mTORC1 hyperactivation). Everolimus directly targets mTORC1; mechanism validated in EXIST-3 (Phase 3, PDUFA 2023).
payload.source: PMID 33626254 (EMERGENT-1, NEJM 2021, muscarinic review); PMID 22496545 (EXIST-3 TSC seizure trial, Lancet 2012); FDA approval summary (everolimus/Afinitor TSC indication)

payload.value: VIABILITY SUMMARY: TSC2 is a **genetically validated target** (loss-of-function mutations → mTORC1 hyperactivation). mTOR inhibitors (everolimus, next-gen PI3K/mTOR duals) are **clinically validated** (approvals, Phase 3 PoC). Competitive set includes 1–2 approved agents (everolimus, epidiolex) + 4–5 clinical-stage programs (Aeovian, Ovid, Filana, Marinus-discontinued, Kazia). **Deal premium**: Kazia licensing suggests $1.5M upfront for early TSC PoC (low PoS implicit); contrast with $14B+ buyouts in schizophrenia (higher PoS signals). TSC orphan-indication economics (45k US patients, ~60% refractory) limit commercial ceiling vs broader CNS indications.
payload.source: Composite of SEC filings + clinical trial registries + deal structure analysis

payload.value: TSC2 gnomAD constraint: pLI 1.00, LOEUF 0.20, missense Z -0.08 (loss-of-function intolerant)
TSC2 GTEx tissue expression (gtex_v8, median TPM): top brain region Brain Cerebellum 133.2; highest-expressing of 54 tissues (CNS-enriched)

TSC2 (UniProt P49815) InterPro: 8 entries — domains: Rap/Ran-GAP domain (IPR000331), Tuberin-type domain (IPR018515), Tuberin, N-terminal (IPR024584); families: Tuberin (IPR003913), Tuberin/Ral GTPase-activating protein subunit alpha (IPR027107)

g:Profiler enrichment for TSC2 (hsapiens): 6 significant terms; top — TSC1-TSC2 complex (GO:0033596, p=3.0e-02); Inhibition of TSC complex formation by PKB (REAC:R-HSA-165181, p=3.7e-02); TSC1-TSC2 complex (CORUM:75, p=5.0e-02); TSC2-HERC1 complex (CORUM:6532, p=5.0e-02); TSC1-TSC2 complex (CORUM:7157, p=5.0e-02)

payload.recommendation: Conditional advance — 3 conditional verdict(s). Address open items before IND filing.

payload.experiment: Run orthogonal in vivo validation for TSC2 in disease-relevant model.
